result.stdout:
Description:
  Hex1b CLI tool for managing and interacting with terminal applications.

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool [command] [options]

Options:
  --json          Output results as JSON
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  terminal     Manage terminal lifecycle, metadata, and connections
  app          TUI application diagnostics (widget tree, focus, state)
  capture      Capture terminal output (screenshots and recordings)
  keys <id>    Send keystrokes to a terminal
  mouse        Send mouse input to a terminal
  assert <id>  Assert on terminal content for scripting and CI
  agent        AI agent integration

commands.payload:
Description:
  Hex1b CLI tool for managing and interacting with terminal applications.

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool [command] [options]

Options:
  --json          Output results as JSON
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  terminal     Manage terminal lifecycle, metadata, and connections
  app          TUI application diagnostics (widget tree, focus, state)
  capture      Capture terminal output (screenshots and recordings)
  keys <id>    Send keystrokes to a terminal
  mouse        Send mouse input to a terminal
  assert <id>  Assert on terminal content for scripting and CI
  agent        AI agent integration

result.stdout:
Description:
  AI agent integration

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool agent [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --json          Output results as JSON

Commands:
  mcp   Start the MCP server (stdio transport)
  init  Initialize Hex1b agent skill file in a repository

result.stdout:
Description:
  Initialize Hex1b agent skill file in a repository

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool agent init [options]

Options:
  --path <path>   Explicit repo root path (skips auto-detection)
  --stdout        Write skill file to stdout instead of disk
  --force         Overwrite existing skill file
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --json          Output results as JSON

Description:
  TUI application diagnostics (widget tree, focus, state)

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool app [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --json          Output results as JSON

Commands:
  tree <id>  Inspect the widget/node tree of a TUI application

commands.payload:

  --json          Output results as JSON

result.stdout:
Description:
  Inspect the widget/node tree of a TUI application

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool app tree <id> [options]

Arguments:
  <id>  Terminal ID (or prefix)

Options:
  --focus          Include focus ring info
  --popups         Include popup stack
  --depth <depth>  Limit tree depth
  --no-perf        Hide performance timing
  -?, -h, --help   Show help and usage information
  --json           Output results as JSON

commands.payload:

  --json           Output results as JSON

result.stdout:
Description:
  Assert on terminal content for scripting and CI

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool assert <id> [options]

Arguments:
  <id>  Terminal ID (or prefix)

Options:
  --text-present <text-present>  Assert text is visible on screen
  --text-absent <text-absent>    Assert text is NOT visible on screen
  --timeout <timeout>            How long to wait in seconds [default: 5]
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information
  --json                         Output results as JSON

result.stdout:
Description:
  Manage asciinema recordings

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool capture recording [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --json          Output results as JSON

Commands:
  start <id>   Start recording a terminal session
  stop <id>    Stop recording a terminal session
  status <id>  Show recording status of a terminal session
  playback     Play back an asciinema recording in the terminal

commands.payload:
Description:
  Manage asciinema recordings

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool capture recording [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --json          Output results as JSON

Commands:
  start <id>   Start recording a terminal session
  stop <id>    Stop recording a terminal session
  status <id>  Show recording status of a terminal session
  playback     Play back an asciinema recording in the terminal

result.stdout:
Description:
  Play back an asciinema recording in the terminal

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool capture recording playback [options]

Options:
  --file <file> (REQUIRED)  Path to .cast file
  --speed <speed>           Playback speed multiplier (default: 1.0) [default: 1]
  --player                  Launch interactive TUI player with controls
  -?, -h, --help            Show help and usage information
  --json                    Output results as JSON

commands.payload:

result.stdout:
Description:
  Start recording a terminal session

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool capture recording start <id> [options]

Arguments:
  <id>  Terminal ID (or prefix)

Options:
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)  Output .cast file path
  --title <title>               Recording title
  --idle-limit <idle-limit>     Max idle time in seconds between frames
  -?, -h, --help                Show help and usage information
  --json                        Output results as JSON

result.stdout:
Description:
  Capture a terminal screen screenshot

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool capture screenshot <id> [options]

Arguments:
  <id>  Terminal ID (or prefix)

Options:
  --format <format>          Output format: text, ansi, svg, html, or png [default: text]
  --output <output>          Save to file instead of stdout (required for png)
  --wait <wait>              Wait for text to appear before capturing
  --timeout <timeout>        Timeout in seconds for --wait [default: 30]
  --scrollback <scrollback>  Number of scrollback lines to include [default: 0]
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information
  --json                     Output results as JSON

result.stdout:
Description:
  Send keystrokes to a terminal

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool keys <id> [options]

Arguments:
  <id>  Terminal ID (or prefix)

Options:
  --key <key>     Named key (Enter, Tab, Escape, F1, UpArrow, etc.)
  --text <text>   Type text as keystrokes
  --ctrl          Ctrl modifier
  --shift         Shift modifier
  --alt           Alt modifier
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --json          Output results as JSON

result.stdout:
Description:
  Send a mouse click at coordinates

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool mouse click <id> <x> <y> [options]

Arguments:
  <id>  Terminal ID (or prefix)
  <x>   Column (0-based)
  <y>   Row (0-based)

Options:
  --button <button>  Mouse button: left, right, or middle [default: left]
  -?, -h, --help     Show help and usage information
  --json             Output results as JSON

result.stdout:
Description:
  Manage terminal lifecycle, metadata, and connections

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool terminal [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --json          Output results as JSON

Commands:
  list             List all known terminals
  start <command>  Start a hosted terminal
  stop <id>        Stop a hosted terminal
  info <id>        Show terminal details
  resize <id>      Resize a terminal
  clean            Remove stale terminal sockets
  attach <id>      Attach to a terminal (Ctrl+] for commands)

commands.payload:
Description:
  Manage terminal lifecycle, metadata, and connections

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool terminal [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --json          Output results as JSON

Commands:
  list             List all known terminals
  start <command>  Start a hosted terminal
  stop <id>        Stop a hosted terminal
  info <id>        Show terminal details
  resize <id>      Resize a terminal
  clean            Remove stale terminal sockets
  attach <id>      Attach to a terminal (Ctrl+] for commands)

result.stdout:
Description:
  Attach to a terminal (Ctrl+] for commands)

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool terminal attach [<id>] [options]

Arguments:
  <id>  Terminal ID (or prefix)

Options:
  --resize               Resize remote terminal to match local terminal dimensions
  --lead                 Claim resize leadership (only the leader's resize events control the remote terminal)
  --web                  Attach via a web browser using xterm.js instead of the TUI
  --web-port <web-port>  Port for the web server (0 for random, default: 0). Only used with --web
  --port <port>          Connect to a WebSocket-exposed host at ws://localhost:{port}/ws/attach
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information
  --json                 Output results as JSON

commands.payload:
Description:
  Attach to a terminal (Ctrl+] for commands)

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool terminal attach [<id>] [options]

Arguments:
  <id>  Terminal ID (or prefix)

Options:
  --resize               Resize remote terminal to match local terminal dimensions
  --lead                 Claim resize leadership (only the leader's resize events control the remote terminal)
  --web                  Attach via a web browser using xterm.js instead of the TUI
  --web-port <web-port>  Port for the web server (0 for random, default: 0). Only used with --web
  --port <port>          Connect to a WebSocket-exposed host at ws://localhost:{port}/ws/attach
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information
  --json                 Output results as JSON

result.stdout:
Description:
  Resize a terminal

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool terminal resize <id> [options]

Arguments:
  <id>  Terminal ID (or prefix)

Options:
  --width <width>    New width in columns
  --height <height>  New height in rows
  -?, -h, --help     Show help and usage information
  --json             Output results as JSON

result.stdout:
Description:
  Start a hosted terminal

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool terminal start [<command>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <command>  Command and arguments to run (after --)

Options:
  --width <width>    Terminal width [default: 120]
  --height <height>  Terminal height [default: 30]
  --cwd <cwd>        Working directory
  --record <record>  Record to asciinema file
  --attach           Immediately attach to the terminal after starting
  --passthru         Run in passthru mode: PTY bridges directly to the current terminal with no chrome
  --port <port>      Port for WebSocket diagnostics listener
  --bind <bind>      Bind address for the WebSocket listener (default: 127.0.0.1, use 0.0.0.0 for containers)
  -?, -h, --help     Show help and usage information
  --json             Output results as JSON

commands.payload:
Description:
  Start a hosted terminal

Usage:
  Hex1b.Tool terminal start [<command>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <command>  Command and arguments to run (after --)

Options:
  --width <width>    Terminal width [default: 120]
  --height <height>  Terminal height [default: 30]
  --cwd <cwd>        Working directory
  --record <record>  Record to asciinema file
  --attach           Immediately attach to the terminal after starting
  --passthru         Run in passthru mode: PTY bridges directly to the current terminal with no chrome
  --port <port>      Port for WebSocket diagnostics listener
  --bind <bind>      Bind address for the WebSocket listener (default: 127.0.0.1, use 0.0.0.0 for containers)
  -?, -h, --help     Show help and usage information
  --json             Output results as JSON
